summary: Recovered the remaining degraded public services after the runtime-pool stabilization work by restoring missing OpenBao-backed runtime env material on `docker-runtime-lv3`, rebuilding the local JupyterHub images, correcting the stale Vaultwarden Tailscale proxy target on `proxmox_florin`, identifying and fixing a branch-local `docker.socket` change that restarted Docker on `runtime-control-lv3`, and replaying Harbor after that control-plane Docker restart left only `harbor-log` running.

verification.check: Targeted Docker runtime and OpenBao helper regression slice
verification.observed: `uv run --with pytest --with pyyaml python -m pytest tests/test_docker_runtime_role.py tests/test_openbao_compose_env_helper.py tests/test_compose_runtime_secret_injection.py -q` passed with `31 passed in 25.22s` after the socket-activation guard was updated to avoid stopping a live Docker daemon.

verification.check: Vaultwarden proxy recovery on runtime-control and the Proxmox host
verification.observed: The replay preserved in `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-04-04-ws-0335-vaultwarden-proxy-recovery-r1.txt` completed successfully with recap `proxmox_florin ok=42 changed=6 failed=0` and `runtime-control-lv3 ok=150 changed=5 failed=0`, and `https://vault.lv3.org/alive` returned `HTTP 200` afterward.

verification.check: Harbor control-plane recovery after the runtime-control Docker restart
verification.observed: The Docker journal captured a `docker.service` restart on `runtime-control-lv3` at `2026-04-04T00:25:30Z`, and the subsequent Harbor replay in `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-04-04-ws-0335-harbor-recovery-r3.txt` restored the full Harbor compose stack; `https://registry.lv3.org/api/v2.0/ping` returned `HTTP 200` afterward.

verification.check: Public lv3.org SLO sweep after the recoveries
verification.observed: The final concurrent sweep of all public `lv3.org` SLO targets returned `200` for every route except the expected Woodpecker `204`, including Directus, Harbor, Matrix Synapse, Outline, JupyterHub, Paperless, Plausible, Vaultwarden, and the remaining public edge contracts.

notes: The branch-local `Disable Docker socket activation` change caused the runtime-control Docker restart because stopping `docker.socket` on a live host implicitly tore down `docker.service`; the role now avoids that outage path by disabling the socket without stopping it when Docker is already active.

Harbor remains sensitive to host-wide Docker restarts because its containers can fail to auto-start when `harbor-log` is not yet accepting logging-driver traffic on `localhost:1514`; the bounded Harbor compose replay is the immediate recovery path when that happens.
The final public SLO sweep is the main green proof for this incident response because it verifies the externally visible contracts instead of only container liveness.

correction_loop.surface: remaining degraded public services after runtime-pool stabilization
correction_loop.repair_action: Restored missing OpenBao-backed runtime env files on `docker-runtime-lv3`, rebuilt and replayed JupyterHub locally, corrected the Vaultwarden proxy drift on `proxmox_florin`, patched the Docker runtime role to stop disabling `docker.socket` by stopping a live Docker daemon, and replayed Harbor after the control-plane Docker restart left the stack partially down.
correction_loop.verification: The final public `lv3.org` sweep returned healthy responses for every public target, with `registry.lv3.org` at `HTTP 200` and `vault.lv3.org` at `HTTP 200`.
